cards.title: Special 1: Payload First
cards.cardSummary: Default Board 1 route. Build chest payload first, cross the wall cleanly, then turn those chests into better rewards with the upgrade zone.
cards.profile: Payload First is the normal Board 1 plan: collect enough chest bodies to matter, solve the trap wall without getting cute, then spend the red upgrade zone on chests you already own.
notes: Build payload first. Cross cleanly. Upgrade immediately.
Five chests is usually enough to cross; six is preferred when the path gives it naturally.
Do not force a weak wall line for one extra small pickup.
After the wall, red upgrades are premium because they can immediately improve owned chests.
Do not over-stage before the wall if you already have payload and a clean crossing line.
cards.useWhen: Use as the default Special Board 1 route.
cards.avoidWhen: Avoid only when the board clearly gives a safer or greedier conversion line.

walkthrough.heading: Chest payload
steps: Before the wall, prioritize chest bodies over empty repositioning. Even green and blue chests can become real value because Board 1 gives immediate red-upgrade access after the trap wall.
Aim for five owned chests as the normal crossing floor. Six is preferred when it does not cost a clean wall line.
Take useful movement and low-life recovery, but do not burn your whole hand trying to land on perfect staging tiles.

walkthrough.heading: Wall timing
steps: Do not force the wall with a tiny payload. The wall is worth crossing when you have enough chest bodies, enough life, and a deterministic movement line.
If the hand naturally solves the trap wall after five or six chests, cross instead of dancing around the pre-wall area.
Preserve offset cards until they either collect payload or create the wall solve.

walkthrough.heading: Upgrade zone
steps: After the wall, red upgrade tiles become the priority. They should be treated as conversion payoff for the chests you already secured.
Use owned upgrade cards and upgrade tiles immediately when they have eligible targets. Do not save upgrades while good owned chest targets exist.
Once the red upgrade chain is exhausted, clean up only if it adds real chest value or safe completion.

metrics.note: Board 1 strategy cards were rewritten after removing the two empty post-wall rows. Final batch metrics should be patched after validation.

cards.title: Special 1: Upgrade Conversion
cards.cardSummary: Greedier Board 1 route. Crosses sooner with enough chest bodies and leans harder into the red upgrade zone for bigger converted rewards.
cards.profile: Upgrade Conversion is the greed route for Board 1. It accepts a smaller chest setup when the wall is clean because the real value comes from reaching the upgrade zone quickly and converting what you already own.
notes: Cross earlier when the upgrade payoff is already lined up.
Four chests can be enough if the wall is clean and upgrades are waiting.
Red upgrades matter more here than perfect chest count.
Higher ceiling, but more variance than Payload First.
Do not call it greed if there is no actual upgrade payoff after the wall.
cards.useWhen: Use when you have enough chest bodies and the wall route is clean.
cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when the wall is messy or your post-wall upgrade payoff is thin.

walkthrough.heading: Conversion setup
steps: This route accepts a smaller payload when the post-wall red upgrade zone is reachable. Four chests can be enough if the wall line is clean and upgrades are waiting.
Do not overfill weak chest slots just to chase 7-chest completion. The goal is fewer better chests, not seven mediocre chests.
Movement cards are premium when they turn a partial payload into immediate upgrade access.

walkthrough.heading: Aggressive wall crossing
steps: Cross earlier than the recommended route when the hand solves the wall and the upgrade zone has enough payoff.
Life buffer is less strict here. A wall toll is acceptable if it unlocks immediate red-upgrade conversion.
Avoid empty pre-wall movement unless it directly creates the crossing line.

walkthrough.heading: Payoff
steps: After the wall, take red upgrades aggressively. They are the reason this route crosses early.
Upgrade owned chests immediately using the highest eligible owned tier first.
Expect more variance: this route can outperform when upgrades chain well, but it may complete seven chests less often.

metrics.note: Board 1 strategy cards were rewritten after removing the two empty post-wall rows. Final batch metrics should be patched after validation.

cards.title: Special 1: Conservative Conversion
cards.cardSummary: Safer Board 1 route. Waits for stronger payload, better life, and a cleaner wall before committing to the upgrade zone.
cards.profile: Conservative Conversion is the low-drama Board 1 plan. It gives up some ceiling to avoid bricking the wall or crossing with too little value to upgrade.
notes: Bigger payload, safer wall, steadier upgrades.
Five chests minimum; six is preferred when available.
Take life when it protects the wall crossing.
Lower speed and lower ceiling, but fewer disaster runs.
Do not sit forever; once the wall is safe and the payload is real, go cash it.
cards.useWhen: Use when the wall is dangerous, life is low, or your hand needs a cleaner setup.
cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when the board offers a clean high-value crossing now.

walkthrough.heading: Safer payload
steps: Build at least five chest bodies before crossing, with six preferred when available.
Take life more seriously than the other Board 1 routes. A small EV loss is acceptable if it prevents a wall brick.
Use movement cards to secure payload and preserve a clean wall line, not to chase low-value empty repositioning.

walkthrough.heading: Safer wall check
steps: Cross only when the line is deterministic and you have a life buffer.
If the wall line is ugly or leaves you near death, keep collecting useful pre-wall value instead.
This route is allowed to be slower because its job is consistency, not maximum ceiling.

walkthrough.heading: Controlled conversion
steps: Once across, red upgrades are still the payoff. Do not ignore upgrade tiles just because this is the safer route.
Use owned upgrade cards as soon as they have eligible targets.
Finish by taking safe remaining value, but do not risk a stabilized board for unnecessary filler.

metrics.note: Board 1 strategy cards were rewritten after removing the two empty post-wall rows. Final batch metrics should be patched after validation.

cards.title: Special 2: Chest Zone Fill
cards.cardSummary: Default Board 2 route. Get through the wall, enter the chest zone, fill reward bodies, and treat upgrades as bonus value instead of the whole plan.
cards.profile: Chest Zone Fill is the normal Board 2 plan now: the chest zone starts right after the wall, so reaching it quickly and filling bodies usually beats waiting around for a perfect red-upgrade bank.
notes: Reach the chest zone. Fill bodies. Use upgrades opportunistically.
The payoff starts immediately after the wall, so access and chest count matter a lot.
Red upgrades are still good, but they are a bonus here, not the main gate.
Less rainbow-focused than Aggro Cross, but steadier and easier to play.
Do not stall before the wall just because another upgrade card might appear.
cards.useWhen: Use as the default Special Board 2 route.
cards.avoidWhen: Avoid only when your hand clearly supports a cleaner high-upgrade Aggro Cross.

walkthrough.heading: Access phase
steps: Before the wall, prioritize movement and life that help you reach the chest zone.
Do not wait for a perfect red-upgrade bank. The corrected board removed the empty gap, so chest payoff starts immediately after the trap wall.
Upgrade tiles before the wall are optional value, not the main gate for this route.

walkthrough.heading: Wall timing
steps: Cross decisively when the line is available and non-lethal.
This route accepts earlier wall entry because the chest zone starts right after the wall.
Avoid wasting moves before the wall if they do not improve access, life safety, or immediate chest payoff.

walkthrough.heading: Chest zone
steps: After the wall, fill chest bodies aggressively. This is where the validated EV came from.
Use banked upgrades when they have eligible secured chest targets, but do not delay chest collection just to create a perfect upgrade setup.
Premium chests are still better, but completion and body count matter more here than in the bank-heavy routes.

metrics.note: Validated best overall EV and chest completion on corrected Board 2.

cards.title: Special 2: Aggro Cross
cards.cardSummary: Greedier Board 2 route. Bank enough upgrades to matter, then cross early to chase higher-quality converted chest outcomes.
cards.profile: Aggro Cross is the high-upgrade Board 2 plan. It does not try to fill every chest; it tries to get across with enough red upgrade value to turn fewer chests into better chests.
notes: Bank enough to matter, then cross before the window closes.
Three to four upgrades can be enough when the wall line is clean.
Better for upgraded/rainbow-style outcomes than raw chest body count.
Higher spike potential, but less consistent than Chest Zone Fill.
Do not cross aggressively if you have no upgrade payoff or no chest access.
cards.useWhen: Use when the wall line is clean and you already have meaningful upgrade payoff.
cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when you need the highest consistency or your upgrade bank is weak.

walkthrough.heading: Fast bank
steps: This route banks enough red-upgrade payoff to matter, but does not wait for a perfect setup.
Three to four upgrades can be enough when the wall line is clean.
Movement that creates wall progress or chest-zone entry is premium.

walkthrough.heading: Aggressive cross
steps: Cross earlier than Bank & Cross when the hand solves the wall and the chest zone is immediately reachable.
The goal is higher-quality converted outcomes, not maximum chest body count.
Do not cross with no payoff: the route is aggressive, not random.

walkthrough.heading: Conversion payoff
steps: Once across, secure chest bodies and spend the bank as soon as eligible targets exist.
This route validated with better median and rainbow output than the slower bank routes, but lower overall EV than Chest Zone Fill.
Expect more variance and fewer filled chests than the recommended route.

metrics.note: Validated high-median and high-rainbow route, but lower total EV than Chest Zone Fill.

cards.title: Special 2: Bank & Cross
cards.cardSummary: Balanced Board 2 route. Bank red upgrades first, cross with a cleaner setup, then convert chests once targets are available.
cards.profile: Bank & Cross is the classic Board 2 plan. It is slower than Chest Zone Fill, but it makes sense when the board naturally gives you red upgrades before the wall without costing a clean chest-zone entry.
notes: Bank upgrades first. Cross cleanly. Convert chests immediately.
Four upgrades is a workable floor; five or more is better when it comes naturally.
Best when upgrades show up without delaying the wall too much.
More patient than Aggro Cross, but can under-fill chest bodies.
Do not keep banking upgrades while a good chest-zone entry is already open.
cards.useWhen: Use when the board naturally gives a strong upgrade bank and clean access.
cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when waiting for more upgrades delays an obvious chest-zone route.

walkthrough.heading: Bank phase
steps: Before the wall, prioritize red upgrade cards and the movement needed to cross.
Four banked upgrades is the normal floor; five or more is preferred when available.
Do not over-collect movement. Movement is access; red upgrades are the payoff.

walkthrough.heading: Wall timing
steps: Cross when you have enough banked upgrades, enough life, and a deterministic or survival-safe wall line.
This route is more patient than Chest Zone Fill and less aggressive than Aggro Cross.
Do not wait forever for a perfect seven-upgrade bank if the chest zone is already available.

walkthrough.heading: Chest zone
steps: After the wall, secure chest targets so the banked upgrades have something to convert.
Spend upgrade cards as soon as they have eligible secured chest targets.
This route is the classic balanced Board 2 plan, but validation showed it is no longer the top EV default on the corrected layout.

metrics.note: Balanced classic bank route; kept as a visible alternative, but no longer default.

cards.title: Special 3: Rainbow/Red High Ceiling
cards.cardSummary: High-ceiling Board 3 route. Chase premium red/rainbow outcomes when the board gives enough setup to make the greed real.
cards.profile: Rainbow Red is the ceiling route for Board 3. It is for hands where premium upgrades and reward access line up, not for forcing greed from a dead board.
notes: Only chase the ceiling when the setup is real.
Premium red/rainbow conversion beats small filler value.
You need access, upgrade payoff, and enough safety to finish the line.
Best spike potential, but easiest to over-force.
Do not tunnel on rainbow if the board is only offering safe completion value.
cards.useWhen: Use when premium upgrade lines and reward access are both available.
cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when the board lacks access, safety, or real upgrade targets.

walkthrough.heading: Setup check
steps: Look for premium upgrade payoff first, not just any red tile.
Make sure you can actually reach the reward targets before committing.
Take movement or safety when it keeps the premium line alive.

walkthrough.heading: Greed window
steps: Commit when the upgrade target and path are both real.
Skip low-value filler if it delays the premium conversion line.
Do not spend correction cards unless they create access or protect the payoff.

walkthrough.heading: Cashout
steps: Once the premium line is secured, finish the route instead of chasing one more maybe-upgrade.
Convert the best eligible rewards first.
If the board turns awkward, fall back to completion instead of bricking the run.

perStartingHand.note: Board 3 is highly hand-size sensitive; extra cards greatly improve access to late red/rainbow bands.

cards.title: Special 3: Red+ Dynamic
cards.cardSummary: Flexible Board 3 route. Prioritizes red-plus style value, but adapts when the board offers safer reward completion instead.
cards.profile: Red Plus Dynamic is the practical Board 3 route. It looks for strong red-plus upgrades, but it is willing to pivot when the board gives completion, access, or safer value instead.
notes: Take red-plus value when it is real; pivot when it is not.
A safe reward line can beat a greedy upgrade that you cannot finish.
Lower ceiling than Rainbow Red, but much less tunnel vision.
Do not keep chasing a red-plus setup after the path stops supporting it.
cards.useWhen: Use when the board has upgrade value, but the best line may change mid-run.
cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when the board clearly demands either pure ceiling greed or pure safe completion.

perStartingHand.note: Balanced EV route also scales strongly with starting hand size.

walkthrough.heading: Read the offer
steps: Start by identifying whether the board is offering red-plus upgrade value or simple reward completion.
Prioritize upgrade targets that you can actually reach and use.
Keep enough movement to pivot if the greedy line dries up.

walkthrough.heading: Dynamic pathing
steps: Take the red-plus line while it is supported by access and targets.
Switch to safe rewards if the upgrade line starts costing too much movement or safety.
Use correction cards for real pivots, not tiny cosmetic improvements.

walkthrough.heading: Finish cleanly
steps: Cash upgraded rewards first, then fill remaining value if the path is safe.
Do not abandon secured value for a late gamble unless the upside is obvious.
A clean finish with good upgrades is better than a bricked ceiling attempt.

cards.title: Special 3: Red+ Softer Fill
cards.cardSummary: Softer Board 3 route. Still looks for red-plus value, but gives more weight to filling reachable rewards and avoiding awkward over-greed.
cards.profile: Red Plus Softer Fill is the steadier version of the red-plus plan. It wants upgrades, but it refuses to ignore easy reward bodies just to chase a fragile perfect line.
notes: Upgrade when good, fill when safe.
Reachable rewards matter more than theoretical perfect upgrades.
Preserve pathing and completion over late greed.
Less spike potential than Dynamic or Rainbow Red, but smoother runs.
Do not skip easy bodies while waiting for an upgrade target that may never line up.
cards.useWhen: Use when the board offers some upgrade value but also plenty of safe reachable rewards.
cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when a clear premium greed route is already open.

perStartingHand.note: Softer Fill keeps completion high while still scaling with hand size.

walkthrough.heading: Build the floor
steps: Secure reachable reward bodies early instead of waiting for a perfect upgrade chain.
Take red-plus value when it lines up naturally.
Preserve movement that keeps multiple reward paths open.

walkthrough.heading: Controlled upgrades
steps: Upgrade secured or easy-to-secure rewards first.
Avoid spending too many cards to force one awkward target.
Use safe filler when it improves the floor without blocking future value.

walkthrough.heading: Close the board
steps: Finish the best reachable rewards instead of chasing late low-odds upgrades.
Take premium upgrades if they remain clean, but do not sacrifice completion for them.
This route wins by avoiding empty greed turns.

cards.title: Special 3: Completion Safe
cards.cardSummary: Safe Board 3 route. Prioritizes reachable rewards, clean pathing, and completion over premium upgrade greed.
cards.profile: Completion Safe is the fallback Board 3 plan. Use it when the upgrade lines are awkward, the hand is clunky, or the board is offering reliable reward bodies instead of clean premium conversions.
notes: Secure reachable value and finish cleanly.
Pathing, safety, reward bodies, then upgrades.
Best when greed lines are too fragile or too expensive.
Lowest ceiling, but strongest anti-brick plan.
Do not turn a safe board into a failed greed board.
cards.useWhen: Use when upgrade lines are awkward, safety is low, or completion value is already available.
cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when the board clearly offers a premium upgrade route with clean access.

perStartingHand.note: Completion Safe scales less explosively because it takes more filler.

walkthrough.heading: Secure access
steps: Take movement and safe reward access before chasing upgrades.
Prefer lines that keep the board finishable.
Do not spend correction cards on greed unless the payoff is already clear.

walkthrough.heading: Fill value
steps: Collect reachable reward bodies and protect the completion route.
Use upgrades as bonus value when they are clean and cheap.
Skip awkward premium targets that threaten the finish.

walkthrough.heading: Finish safely
steps: Close the board with the best secured rewards.
Avoid late pivots that strand movement or break the path.
The goal is a reliable cashout, not a highlight roll.
